mission.title: Install and Configure Argo CD Operator
mission.description: The Argo CD Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to manage Argo CD clusters efficiently. It automates the deployment and management of Argo CD instances, making it easier to implement GitOps workflows in Kubernetes environments. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes operators and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Argo CD Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Argo CD Operator Helm repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add argocd-operator https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Argo CD Operator in the 'argocd' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

helm install argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd --create-namespace --version 0.17.0

```

> Ensure the namespace is created if it doesn't exist.

steps.title: Create the CatalogSource
steps.description:
This step creates a CatalogSource resource that points to the Argo CD Operator image registry.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

```

> This CatalogSource is necessary for the operator to function.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

steps.title: Create the OperatorGroup
steps.description:
This command creates an OperatorGroup resource to define the scope of the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
EOF

```

> This allows the operator to manage resources in the specified namespace.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
EOF

steps.title: Create the Subscription
steps.description:
This step creates a Subscription resource to manage the lifecycle of the Argo CD Operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

```

> This ensures the operator is installed and updated automatically.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Argo CD Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd

```

> You should see the Argo CD Operator pod in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Argo CD Operator
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Argo CD Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Argo CD instances are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall argocd-operator --namespace argocd

kubectl delete namespace argocd

```

> This will remove the operator but not the Argo CD instances.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Argo CD Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any critical data. The upgrade process involves uninstalling the current version and installing the new version.

```bash

helm upgrade argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd --version 0.17.0

kubectl get pods -n argocd

```

> This command upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the new version is running correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "CatalogSource" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OLM (Operator Lifecycle Manager) is not installed or not running.

**Fix:**
Install OLM by following the official installation guide.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The API version specified in the YAML files is incorrect or deprecated.

**Fix:**
Check the API versions in the YAML files and update them to the latest supported versions.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or missing permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n argocd' and correct any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found' when checking pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Argo CD Operator may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the operator and ensure all steps were followed correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-cluster management and improved RBAC configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.16.0, ensure to review the new RBAC settings and adjust your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the Argo CD Operator pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n argocd'.

kind: CatalogSource

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
